meta.title: About — Mohammed Nasser Al-Qahtani
meta.description: Strategy, Performance, and Transformation professional with more than 21 years across strategy execution, transformation, performance management, governance, and change management.

hero.headline: A practitioner, sharing what I've learned.
hero.subline: Strategy, Performance, and Transformation professional with more than 21 years of experience across strategy execution, organizational transformation, performance management, governance, and change management.

bio.title: My background.
bio.p1: I'm Mohammed Nasser Al-Qahtani. For more than 21 years I've worked on strategy, execution, and organizational transformation — the practical, often unglamorous work of helping institutions move from plans to results.
bio.p2: My focus areas are strategy and strategy execution, organizational transformation, change management, performance management, governance, and leadership. I care less about frameworks for their own sake, and more about what holds up under real conditions.
bio.p3: Along the way I completed an M.Sc. in Management, Strategy & Leadership at Michigan State University, executive programs at Harvard Business School, and certifications in change management (Prosci) and performance (KPI Institute) — and I've worked alongside firms like Bain & Company, PwC, and Deloitte. This site is where I share what I've learned: a knowledge platform, not a résumé.

philosophy.title: What I believe.
philosophy.quote: A strategy is only as good as an organization's ability to execute it. My work begins where most strategies stall — turning intent into governed, measurable results that endure.

strategyToResults.title: From strategy to measurable results.
strategyToResults.subtitle: Six stages, one connected system — built from practice, not theory. It maps where strategy usually loses momentum, and how to carry it through to results.
strategyToResults.metaDescription: A practical framework connecting strategy, execution, change, adoption, performance, and results — built from real-world experience.
strategyToResults.intro: Most strategies don't fail at the design stage — they fail in the space between intent and impact. This framework comes from years of working in that gap: six stages treated as one connected system, not separate hand-offs.

stages.name: Strategy
stages.body: A clear, ownable direction the whole organization can act on.

stages.name: Execution
stages.body: Turning intent into disciplined, governed delivery.

stages.name: Change
stages.body: Leading people through the change the strategy demands.

stages.name: Adoption
stages.body: Embedding new ways of working so they hold.

stages.name: Performance
stages.body: Measuring what matters and improving continuously.
